% cube_t6_pell_simplify_tex.txt (Pell-simplified TeXForms produced by
% Mathematica/CubeT6PellSimplifyV2.wl).
% These are the same 12 independent scalars as before, but with
% Pell-identity substitutions applied:
%   ArcCosh[A_k(\sqrt{3})] -> k Log[2+\sqrt{3}]   for k = 1..16
%   ArcCosh[A_k(\sqrt{2})] -> k Log[1+\sqrt{2}]   for even k = 2..12
%   Log[(2+\sqrt{3})^k composite] -> k Log[2+\sqrt{3}], etc.
% followed by Together + ExpandAll + FullSimplify (with Reals assumptions).
% Each simplified expression has been verified numerically against the
% original WorkingPrecision->30 value to >25 digits.
% LeafCount drops from O(10^3) (raw) to O(10^2) (Pell-simplified).

\subsection{Closed-form symbolic expressions for the scalars}
\label{sec:results-closedforms}

The closed forms below are produced by
\texttt{Mathematica/CubeT6OvernightHighPrec.wl} via the hybrid
iterated--octant symbolic strategy (Strategy~1) followed by
\texttt{FullSimplify} with Pell-identity rules
$\{(2+\sqrt{3})^{k}\to A_k+B_k\sqrt{3}\}_{k=1}^{16}$ and
$\{(1+\sqrt{2})^{k}\to A_k+B_k\sqrt{2}\}_{k=2,4,\dots,12}$, applied via
\texttt{Mathematica/CubeT6PellSimplifyV2.wl}.  The Pell pass collapses the
recurring atoms $\cosh^{-1}(189750626)\to 15\log(2+\sqrt{3})$,
$\cosh^{-1}(2)\to\log(2+\sqrt{3})$,
$\sinh^{-1}(1)\to\log(1+\sqrt{2})$,
$\coth^{-1}(\sqrt{2})\to\log(1+\sqrt{2})$,
$\coth^{-1}(\sqrt{3})\to\log(1+\sqrt{3})-\tfrac{1}{2}\log 2$,
$\tanh^{-1}(1/\sqrt{2})\to\log(1+\sqrt{2})$,
$\log(7\pm 4\sqrt{3})\to\pm 2\log(2+\sqrt{3})$,
and similar collapses, into the closed atom basis below.  The single
remaining unreducible atom is the awkward
$\coth^{-1}(29681\sqrt{3}/51409)$ in $\alpha_1$, whose argument
does not match any clean Pell pair.

\paragraph{Atom basis.}
After the Pell pass every expression is built from
\begin{multline*}
\Bigl\{\,1,\ \pi,\ \sqrt{2},\ \sqrt{3},\ \log 2,\ \log(1+\sqrt{2}),\
\log(1+\sqrt{3}),\ \log(2+\sqrt{3}),\\
\tan^{-1}(\cdot),\ \coth^{-1}\!\bigl(\tfrac{29681\sqrt{3}}{51409}\bigr),\
\tanh^{-1}(\cdot)\,\Bigr\},
\end{multline*}
where the $\tan^{-1}$ and remaining $\tanh^{-1}$ arguments are the
small-rational arctangents
\[
\{3/8,\ 5/8,\ 7/16,\ 5/16,\ 21/32,\ 35/48,\ 35/64\}
\]
and their $\sqrt{3}$-conjugate partners that arise from the polynomial
arctangent reductions on the cube faces.

\medskip
\noindent
Each equation is typeset with \verb|\resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{...}|
so the long expressions fit the page width.  The numerical
value in parentheses is the \texttt{WorkingPrecision}$\,\to\,30$
evaluation from Table~\ref{tab:high-prec-scalars}.  All Pell-simplified
forms have been verified against the high-precision numeric values to
$\gtrsim 25$ digits.
